product_overview: RAG ChatBot Học Tập is a role-based learning platform where lecturers manage courses and course materials, and students ask AI questions grounded in those materials. The app also includes quizzes, subscriptions, and admin tools for managing users, payments, and system activity.
core_goals: Sign in and access the app according to user role
Create and manage courses and course lecturers
Upload and manage course documents for study use
Ask course-grounded questions and review cited answers and chat history
Create, take, and review quizzes tied to course content
View and upgrade to Premium subscription and inspect personal payment history
Manage users, whitelists, payments, and application logs as an administrator

features.name: Authentication and role routing
features.description: Users sign in with local credentials and are routed into the appropriate Admin, Lecturer, or Student area. Authenticated users can also end their session from the app.
user_flows: Navigate to /Account/Login -> Enter username and password -> Submit the login form -> See the app redirect to the role-appropriate area
Navigate to /Account/Login -> Enter invalid credentials -> Submit the login form -> See validation feedback
Navigate to /Account/Login -> Start Google login -> Complete external sign-in -> See the app redirect to the role-appropriate area
Access the authenticated navigation -> Log out -> See the session end and return to the public entry experience

features.name: Course administration and lecturer assignment
features.description: Administrators can view the course list, create and update course information, assign or replace the lecturer, and delete courses after confirmation.
user_flows: Navigate to /Courses -> View and search the course list -> Create a course -> See the new course appear in the list
Navigate to /Courses -> Open an existing course for editing -> Update course metadata -> Save changes -> See the updated course information
Navigate to /Courses -> Select a course -> Assign or replace the lecturer -> Save changes -> See the lecturer assignment reflected in the course list
Navigate to /Courses -> Choose a course for removal -> Confirm deletion -> See the course disappear from the list

features.name: Course documents and indexing
features.description: Assigned lecturers manage course documents, monitor processing status, inspect extracted chunks, download available files, and trigger document operations for their own courses. Students can only view approved material.
user_flows: Navigate to /Courses -> Open a course's documents page -> View document processing status and available documents
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Documents -> Upload a supported document as the assigned lecturer -> See the document appear with processing status
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Documents -> Open a document's details -> Inspect extracted chunks -> Download the document
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Documents -> Approve a document as the assigned lecturer -> See the document become available for use
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Documents -> Retry, delete, or reindex course documents as the assigned lecturer -> See the document list and processing state update

features.name: Course-grounded chat and history
features.description: Authenticated users can ask questions about a selected course, read cited answers, track remaining quota, and revisit previous chat threads and messages.
user_flows: Navigate to /Courses -> Open the chat panel -> Select a course -> Send a question -> Read the answer with sources and remaining quota
Navigate to /Courses -> Open the chat panel -> Select a course -> Send a question longer than allowed -> See validation feedback before submission
Navigate to /Courses -> Open the chat panel -> Select a course with no available context -> Send a question -> See graceful feedback that the course is missing or unavailable
Navigate to /Courses -> Open prior chat threads -> Reopen a thread -> Review the stored messages and answers

features.name: Quiz management and attempts
features.description: Assigned lecturers can create quizzes and generate questions, while students can start attempts, submit answers, and review results and attempt history.
user_flows: Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Quiz -> View available quizzes and source documents
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Quiz -> Create a quiz as the assigned lecturer -> Add or edit questions -> Save the quiz -> See it listed for the course
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Quiz -> Generate quiz questions as the assigned lecturer -> Review generated questions -> Save the quiz content
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Quiz -> Start a quiz attempt as a student -> Select answers -> Submit the attempt -> See the score and correct answers
Navigate to /Courses/{CourseCode}/Quiz -> Open attempt history -> Review past attempts and results

features.name: Premium subscription and personal transaction history
features.description: Users can compare Free and Premium plans, start Premium checkout, handle successful or cancelled payment return states, and review only their own transaction history.
user_flows: Navigate to /Subscription -> Compare the Free and Premium plans
Navigate to /Subscription -> Start Premium checkout -> Continue through payment flow -> Return from a successful payment -> See the subscription state update
Navigate to /Subscription -> Start Premium checkout -> Cancel the payment -> Return to the cancelled state page
Navigate to /Account/Transactions -> Open personal transaction history -> Review the user's own payment records

features.name: Admin dashboard and payment filtering
features.description: Administrators can review dashboard totals and charts, adjust reporting period, and inspect payment records with type and status filters.
user_flows: Navigate to /Admin/Dashboard -> View totals and charts -> Change the reporting period or year -> See the dashboard data refresh
Navigate to /Admin/Dashboard -> Open the payment list -> Review transaction records -> Filter transactions by type and status
Navigate to /Admin/Dashboard -> Attempt to access as a non-admin user -> See access denied

features.name: Admin user and whitelist management
features.description: Administrators can create users, change subscription tiers, delete accounts, import users, and manage whitelist entries for student access.
user_flows: Navigate to /Admin/Users -> Create an account with role and subscription tier -> See the new user appear in the list
Navigate to /Admin/Users -> Change a user's subscription tier between Free and Premium -> See the updated tier reflected
Navigate to /Admin/Users -> Select an account for removal -> Confirm deletion -> See the account removed from the list
Navigate to /Admin/Users -> Import users from a spreadsheet -> See the imported users added to the system
Navigate to /Admin/Whitelist -> Add, delete, or import whitelist entries -> See the whitelist list update

features.name: Admin log inspection
features.description: Administrators can select a log file, inspect its contents, and clear the selected log after confirmation.
user_flows: Navigate to /Admin/Logs -> Select a log file -> Inspect the log contents
Navigate to /Admin/Logs -> Select a log file -> Confirm clearing the log -> See the selected log emptied

tech_stack: Entity Framework Core 9 with PostgreSQL and pgvector
Cookie authentication with optional Google OAuth
Gemini-compatible AI APIs for chat, embeddings, and quiz generation

routes.description: Landing page that redirects authenticated users to their role-specific area
Username/password and Google login page
Ends the current cookie-authenticated session
Shows the current user's payment transaction history
Role-aware course list; administrators manage courses and lecturer assignments
Lecturer/student document page with lecturer-only document management actions
Lecturer quiz management and student quiz-taking page
Subscription plan selection page
Creates a PayOS checkout session for Premium
Verifies a successful PayOS return and updates payment/subscription state
Handles a cancelled PayOS checkout
Admin-only metrics and revenue dashboard
Admin-only user creation, deletion, import, role, and tier management
Admin-only student email whitelist management and spreadsheet import
Admin-only payment list with transaction-type and status filtering
Admin-only application log viewer and log clearing page
Authenticated JSON endpoint for course-grounded chat messages
Authenticated JSON endpoint for chat thread and message history
Lecturer/student JSON handlers for quiz management, attempts, and review
PayOS webhook endpoint with provider signature verification

features.name: Authentication and role routing
features.description: Users sign in with local credentials or Google and are redirected according to Admin, Lecturer, or Student role.
user_interactions: Enter username and password
Submit the login form
See validation feedback for invalid credentials
Optionally start Google OAuth login
Log out from the authenticated navigation

features.name: Course administration and lecturer assignment
features.description: Admin can create, view, edit, delete courses, assign a lecturer, and replace the assigned lecturer without entering document, indexing, or quiz management.
user_interactions: View and search the course list
Create a course
Edit course metadata
Assign or change the lecturer
Delete a course after confirmation
Verify admin cannot open deep document or quiz management pages

features.name: Course documents and indexing
features.description: Assigned lecturers manage course documents while students have read-only access to approved material.
user_interactions: View document processing status
Upload a supported document as an assigned lecturer
Approve, retry, delete, or reindex documents as an assigned lecturer
Inspect extracted chunks
Download an accessible document
Verify student and admin mutation controls are hidden and server-protected

features.name: Course-grounded chat and history
features.description: Authenticated users ask questions against a selected course, consume daily quota, view sources, and reopen their own chat threads.
user_interactions: Open the chat panel
Select a course
Send a question up to 4000 characters
Read the answer, remaining quota, and document sources
Browse prior threads and reopen their messages
See graceful feedback for missing course, exhausted quota, or unavailable AI

features.name: Quiz management and attempts
features.description: Assigned lecturers create or AI-generate quizzes; students start, submit, and review attempts for accessible courses.
user_interactions: View available quizzes and source documents
Create or delete a quiz as an assigned lecturer
Add, edit, delete, or AI-generate questions as an assigned lecturer
Start a quiz attempt as a student
Select answers and submit the attempt
Review score, answers, and attempt history

features.name: Premium subscription and personal transaction history
features.description: Users view plan information, start Premium checkout, process return/cancel states, and inspect only their own transaction history.
user_interactions: Compare Free and Premium plans
Start checkout for Premium
Return from a successful or cancelled payment
Open personal transaction history
Verify another user's transaction data is never displayed

features.name: Admin dashboard and payment filtering
features.description: Admin reviews metrics and filters transactions by transaction type and payment status.
user_interactions: View dashboard totals and charts
Change dashboard period and year
Open the payment list
Filter transactions by type and status
Verify non-admin users receive access denied

features.name: Admin user and whitelist management
features.description: Admin creates/deletes users, changes subscription tier, imports users, and manages whitelisted student emails.
user_interactions: Create an account with role and subscription tier
Toggle a user between Free and Premium
Delete an account after confirmation
Import users from a spreadsheet
Add, delete, or import whitelist entries

features.name: Admin log inspection
features.description: Admin reads application logs and can clear a selected log file.
user_interactions: Select a log file
Inspect log contents
Clear a selected log after confirmation

known_limitations.issue: The isolated TestSprite environment uses intentionally invalid credentials for Google OAuth, Supabase Storage, PayOS, email, and AI providers.
known_limitations.impact: External login, real checkout, file upload/storage, outbound email, AI chat, embeddings, and AI quiz generation should fail safely and are not valid end-to-end success assertions in this run.

known_limitations.issue: The fresh test database initially contains demo Admin and Lecturer accounts but no courses, documents, quizzes, transactions, or Student account.
known_limitations.impact: Tests must create prerequisite records through the UI before exercising dependent workflows.

known_limitations.issue: Admin deep access to document, indexing, and quiz management is intentionally forbidden; Admin is limited to course CRUD and lecturer assignment in the course area.
known_limitations.impact: An Admin access-denied response on document or quiz routes is expected behavior, not a defect.

known_limitations.issue: Premium chat is limited to 50 questions per day and Free chat to 10 questions per day.
known_limitations.impact: Repeated chat tests consume quota in the isolated database and should assert the limit rather than assume unlimited usage.
